SourceString: Thank you for playing the Bladesong Demo!
This short tutorial will teach you all you need to know. Please follow it closely.
Welcome to the camp, swordsmith!
People will come to you with <emph>commissions</>.
You choose which commissions to take on.
Commissions refresh daily.
Select the highlighted button to choose your first commission.
When you accept a commission, you prepare a billet for your sword.
Preparing this billet costs <emph>Action Points</> <AP/>.
Action Points <AP/> replenish daily.
Preparing this billet also costs <emph>steel</> <Steel/>.
To rotate the camera, hold the <emph>right mouse button</> and move the mouse.
To move the camera along the blade, hold the <emph>middle mouse button</> or <emph>Alt + right mouse button</> and move the mouse.
To zoom in or out, use the <emph>mouse wheel</> or hold <emph>Ctrl + right mouse button</> and move the mouse.
Use the tutorial flag in the top bar to revisit any information you’ve already seen.
Customers have different <emph>requirements</> which your sword must fulfil.
The top left panel shows your progress towards commission requirements.
Hover the cursor over the Blade Length requirement to see helpful details.
Let’s start shaping your blade.
Click the <AddAbility/> button to add a <ability>Change Length</> ability.
You can apply light or heavy hammer hits to alter your blade.
Let’s start with light hits. Click on the steel billet to hammer and lengthen it.
In order to make larger changes on your blade, you can build up strength with heavy hits.
Click when the hammer is at its highest position for a heavy hit.
Hammer in time with the rhythm for stronger hits.
The longer your chain of heavy hits, the more your blade changes with each hit.
Now that the Blade Length requirement is met, let’s flatten the blade.
Click the <AddAbility/> button to add a <ability>Change Thickness</> ability.
Feel free to use light or heavy hits as you please.
Hold shift for even lighter hammer hits.
Use <key>X</> to flip the hammering direction.
Let’s make the blade wider using the <ability>Change Width</> ability.
Hammer a point on the sword.
Usually, customers won’t accept a sword without a point.
The <ability>Cross-Section</> ability makes your blade sharp. Let’s add it.
This ability has two steps. First, you need to give the blade a sharp edge.
Watch the cross-section display in the top right to get a better understanding of what’s changing.
Hammer until the blue line on the blade turns orange.
The edge is now sharp and you can further adjust the shape of the cross-section.
Keep hammering until the hexagon shape <Hexagon/> becomes a diamond shape <Diamond/>.
Great, your blade is complete!
Click the <emph>hilt mode</> button to start working on the hilt.
In hilt mode, you have access to a library of sword parts to complete your sword.
Select a guard from the library, then attach it to a slot on your sword.
In hilt mode, attaching parts costs <emph>resources</>.
Keep an eye on the resource panel to see how much you’re spending.
Add a grip part next.
Your customer requires a two-handed sword, so your grip section needs to be long enough.
Attach a second grip part to the first.
When holding a sword part, you can cycle through them using the <key>Q</>/<key>E</> or Ctrl + scroll wheel.
Select a pommel and try this now.
Every part of your sword influences its <emph>physics</>.
Watch the weight and balance meters shift as you attach a pommel.
You can detach sword parts again up by clicking on them.
You will get your resources back, so feel free to experiment.
When you are happy with your two-handed hilt, let’s make some final adjustments to the blade.
When your hilt is complete with a guard, two grip pieces and a pommel, let’s make some final adjustments to the blade.
The ability history shows all abilities that have been used on the blade.
You can use this to revisit abilities and edit or delete them.
The number next to an applied ability shows its intensity.
Editing abilities has no cost. Feel free to experiment.
Select the <ability>Change Width</> ability you added earlier.
Press <key>X</> to flip the direction of your hammering, then hammer to narrow the blade.
Perfect!
This commission is fulfilled, so it’s time to sell the sword. Click the button above.
Congratulations on finishing your first sword!
Another customer awaits.
Customers with <emph>repair jobs</> will provide you with a sword that they want edited.
These commissions don’t have an initial billet cost, so they’re perfect for making some coin when you’re low on resources.
This customer wants his sword altered. The rounded point needs to be turned into a narrow point.
Revisit the <ability>Point</> ability to reforge it.
You can change the <emph>range</> of many abilities.
The range influenced by your ability is marked with a line.
Drag the circular handle at the end of this line.
Hammer to apply your change.
Nice work!
When you’ve fulfilled all requirements, the commission is complete and you can sell the sword.
You just received a <emph>scrap item</>!
Go to your <emph>inventory</> to inspect what you received.
<emph>Salvaging</> turns scrap items into useful resources.
This costs a small amount of Action Points <AP/>, so it’s a perfect use for leftover points at the end of a day.
Hover over items to see descriptions.
Interact with the item to salvage it.
When your Action Points <AP/> are spent, it’s time to <emph>end the day</>.
If you end the day with any Action Points <AP/> left, they will be lost, so try to use them up whenever you can.
Nicely done – you finished the tutorial!
From today on, you’ll have more <AP/> to spend. You won’t be able to complete every commission, so pick the ones you like the sound of.
The game will open up from here. Don’t be afraid to explore!
Here are a few resources to get you started.
You just unlocked your first <emph>vendor</>!
At any point during the day, visit vendors to buy resources and other items.
Use the asymmetry feature to create single-edged blades or add decoration to one side of the sword.
For most abilities, you can set which sides of the blade are influenced – left, right or both.
Click the asymmetry button to cycle through.
Some abilities, like <ability>Cross-Section</>, feature advanced controls that allow you to further customise their shape.
Once your ability reaches 100%, advanced controls become available.
Select and hammer to change various shape parameters.
This customer has a <emph>style requirement</>.
Customers may ask for sword parts from a certain book. Relevant parts are marked.
Customers may also ask for certain materials or colours.
Apply parts and materials to meet the customer’s requirements.
You are now able to <emph>scale</> sword parts.
Pick up any sword part, then hold <key>W</> and <key>S</> to scale the part.
Scaling influences resource usage as well as the part’s weight – a handy tool for balancing your sword’s physics.
You now have the ability to <emph>stretch and bend</> sword parts.
Drag the handle at the end of a part to stretch and bend it, or hold shift to keep it straight.
Stretching and bending influences resource usage as well as the part’s weight.
You have used up all your resources! To complete this sword, salvage more resources or buy them at a vendor.
Did you know you can <emph>rename</> your sword? Click the name in the top bar and start typing.
You have earned a <emph>skill point</>! Open the skill tree to spend it on improvements.
You have gained <emph>reputation with a faction</>! Select the highlighted button to see how to reach the next rank.
This commission requires a <emph>hand-and-a-half</> grip.
Place a grip part, then grab the handle at the end to make it longer or shorter.
Use one long or two short grip parts to achieve a hand-and-a-half grip.
